oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Stop chasing bugs: Managing outbreaks from a systems perspective
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Hilton, W. Mark
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: history
diagnosis
systems thinking
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Beef cattle veterinary medicine, and likely most aspects of veterinary medicine, consist of a complex system where many items influence an outcome. The technique I use when exam­ining individual animals or herd-level issues is to explore 7 pillars of beef cattle practice and assess how they may affect the “health” of the animal, herd or business. An additional benefit of using a systems approach is that all areas are ex­amined, minimizing the risk that an important aspect will be overlooked.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 23-24
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
